message:
Welcome to Version Confusion, the responsibility is on you to size up the problem.

? *Use `/help` at any time for a list of useful links and commands*
 ? *Use `/incident` to create an incident record*

Once youre ready just click start to begin
Hey guys im seeing an issue with the website loading slowly
Anyone else experaning this?
No all ok for me
No issue for me
What browser and version of it are you using Tanya?
Im using chrome and let me check the version
Ok if it is V105.0 this is a recorded bug with a ticket in the backlog
Yeah it is V105.0
Should we not raise an incident for this? I know I can update the version and it will stop effecting me.
But could this not be effecting customers?
Had a look at the market share of people using this version it is only at 0.09%
Still not sure if it should not be an incidnt. @ramesh.matta-dominos.co.uk What do you think?
HI @bob , May I know the impact on the issue that is reported website is loading slow ?
:star: Asking questions to validate impact of the problem
I'll check the reports now
I do have a few reports from users complaining about timeouts and slow load times
Please let me know if this is happening on all regions or only particular region?
The reports I see right now aren't coming from one specific region
I don't see the point in raising a ticket for a simple bug
May I know the impact of this on Business and Customers?
Ohh the reports is actually showing that 20% of our customers are experiencing an issue where it is taking three times as long the website to load. Seeing a few timeouts occurring as well
It appears this has been ongoing for over two weeks as the reports date that far back. I can also see that there has been a drop in sales of 10% compared to the average over a two week period
We logged it as a bug back then, we've just had a lot of feature work this sprint
Incident p1 website experiencing slowness impacting 20% customers
:bulb: Declare an incident.
:star: Qualify the problem
Incident p2 website experiencing slowness impacting 20% customers
help
Use /incident followed by (description) to formally declare an incident
*Incident Number INC100095:* P2 "website experiencing slowness impacting 20% customers"
:star: Incident record created
Really dont think we need an incident. As I said we have a bug already and really need to focus on delivery of new features.
Hi Daniel, Can you please help me to analyze why this is causing ?
HI @shay , Please analyze why the website is causing the delay?
I have no idea what's causing the issue
would you like to see the ticket logged from two weeks ago?
I'll help where I can!
Want to see the changes made recently?
HI @bez and @Tinus, There is a P2 issue created for website slowness impacting 20% customers, we are analysing the issue..
:star: Formally assign criticality level to the incident
I will comeback in 5 mins and keep you posted
:star: Track actions and time box them
Can you confirm it's a global issue?
Are there any deployments happened any Scripts that are ran before this?
:star: Check changes done to the platform
Not sure about scripts but there were some deployments today
Here you go: https://uptime-labs.atlassian.net/wiki/spaces/ULPUBLIC/pages/1114341401/Version+Confusion+-+Change+Request+Logs
HI Daniel and Shay, I need update with in 2 mins immediately to root cause of this?

Title 
Slow load times for users using V105.0 of chrome
Description

User have reported the frontend as loading slowly and with quick look at there set up appears to be an issue with frontend for users using V105.0 of chrome. 
Reporter
User
Components
OB-Frontend
This is the ticket created
Also where do we even begin to investigate you're the IM
Bez wants an update on the investigation, what do we know so far?
HI @tanya,Daniel  , I see CHG89553 , May I know the details on this?
I believe that's Daniels change
Can we roll back this CHG89553 ?
Yes, i was just doing some clean-up of the code
:star: Restoration over root cause analysis
Do you think the issue is related to my change>
I have no issue rolling it back, just wondering why you think it's the cause
Yes, it could be .. Please analyze and let me know what else can impact this?
I'll begin the rollback first
Rollback has been completed
HI @shay , Please check verify now after the roll back..
HI Daniel, please verify now and let me know..
Erm I wasn't having an issues accessing the site
Yes, it is . we are trying the find the root cause..
I will keep you posted in few mins..
:bulb: Restoration over root cause analysis
We need to focus on restoring the service to those impacted, what do we know so far?
HI Daniel/Tanya, Is the slowness issue resolved?
I'm still having the slowness issue with intermittent timeouts
HI Hamed, can you please help me if there is any other issue ?
I never had any issues with the site
I would like to add I'm using chrome version 105.0
HI Daniel, Slowness is observed as per Tanya. Request you to analyze the root cause..

Thank you for playing Version Confusion, We recommend that you take 5 minutes to go and get a drink or stretch your legs.
Once you are back we ask that you complete this retrospective and feedback form, just hit start when you are ready.
Tanya, Are you saying it is only related to browser?
Thank you for playing! The drill has been completed.

Thanks for playing! Please complete your feedback here:
https://learn.uptimelabs.io/scenario/online-boutique/levels/17/7774/feedback
